report_metadata.stage: Stage IIIC (T4bN2cM0), ulcerated, with in-transit metastases

treatments.name: Neoadjuvant Nivolumab plus Ipilimumab Followed by Surgery

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 NADINA trial: 12-month EFS 83.7% vs 57.2% with adjuvant nivolumab alone; 68% reduction in recurrence/death; 2-year EFS 77.3% vs 55.7%; practice-changing results now standard of care for resectable stage III

survival_benefit.rationale: 2-year EFS 77.3% with neoadjuvant vs 55.7% adjuvant; 2-year DMFS 82.8% vs 63.9%; 59% major pathological response rate; patients with MPR had 95.1% 12-month RFS; transformative outcome improvement

accessibility.rationale: Both nivolumab and ipilimumab FDA approved; neoadjuvant approach now endorsed by NCCN 2025 guidelines; requires only 2 cycles pre-surgery

safety_profile.rationale: Immune-related adverse events with combined checkpoint inhibition; grade 3/4 AEs in ~30%; hepatitis, colitis, endocrinopathies; short duration (2 cycles) limits cumulative toxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: Effective regardless of BRAF status in NADINA; IFN-gamma signature, TMB, and PD-L1 predictive; scalp melanoma's higher TMB may enhance response; ulcerated melanomas may respond better to immunotherapy

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Combined PD-1 (nivolumab) and CTLA-4 (ipilimumab) checkpoint blockade activates both effector and regulatory T-cell compartments, generating robust anti-tumor immunity. Neoadjuvant administration in the presence of intact tumor provides maximal antigen exposure for immune priming, generating superior systemic anti-tumor immunity compared to adjuvant therapy post-resection.

treatments.name: Adjuvant Pembrolizumab (Post-Resection)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 KEYNOTE-054: 7-year RFS 50% vs 36% placebo; 5-year DMFS 60.6% vs 44.5%; consistent benefit across BRAF mutant and WT, PD-L1 positive and negative subgroups

survival_benefit.rationale: Sustained 7-year RFS benefit with HR 0.61; 5-year DMFS HR 0.62; benefit in stage IIIC subgroup; prevents distant metastases and improves long-term disease control

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved adjuvant treatment for resected stage III melanoma; widely available; standard 1-year treatment course (18 doses Q3W)

safety_profile.rationale: Monotherapy better tolerated than combination immunotherapy; grade 3/4 AEs ~15%; no significant long-term QoL impact per KEYNOTE-054 QoL analysis

biomarker_match.rationale: Effective in both BRAF V600E mutant and WT melanoma; benefit in PD-L1 positive and negative; consistent across stage IIIA-IIIC subgroups

treatments.mechanism_of_action: PD-1 checkpoint inhibitor that blocks the interaction between PD-1 on T-cells and PD-L1/PD-L2, restoring anti-tumor T-cell activity. Adjuvant administration after complete surgical resection targets micrometastatic disease and prevents recurrence through sustained immune surveillance.

treatments.name: Adjuvant Dabrafenib plus Trametinib (12 Months)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 COMBI-AD final results (2024): HR 0.52 for RFS, HR 0.56 for DMFS at nearly 10-year follow-up; HR 0.75 for death in BRAF V600E subset; FDA approved adjuvant therapy

survival_benefit.rationale: RFS HR 0.52 sustained at 10 years; DMFS HR 0.56; 20% lower risk of death (HR 0.75) in BRAF V600E patients; durable benefit maintained long after 12-month treatment completion

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for adjuvant treatment of resected stage III BRAF V600E/K melanoma; oral medications; standard 12-month treatment course

safety_profile.rationale: Pyrexia (febrile reactions) in ~50%; fatigue, nausea, headache; dose modifications common; cardiac toxicity rare but monitored; 12-month fixed duration limits exposure

biomarker_match.rationale: BRAF V600E mutation is the direct drug target; patient has confirmed BRAF V600E making this a precision medicine approach; dedicated COMBI-AD V600E subgroup analysis shows significant benefit

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Dabrafenib inhibits BRAF V600E kinase blocking constitutive activation of MAPK/ERK pathway. Trametinib inhibits MEK1/2 downstream, preventing pathway reactivation and resistance. Combined BRAF+MEK inhibition provides complete pathway blockade, eradicating micrometastatic BRAF V600E-mutant melanoma cells after surgical resection.

treatments.name: Adjuvant Nivolumab (Post-Resection)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 CheckMate 238: 9-year follow-up showing median RFS 61.1 months vs 24.2 months with ipilimumab; 5-year RFS 50% vs 39%; well-established standard of care

survival_benefit.rationale: Median RFS 61.1 months vs 24.2 months (HR 0.76); 5-year RFS 50%; sustained long-term benefit with 9-year follow-up data confirming durability

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for adjuvant treatment of resected stage IIIB-C and IV melanoma; widely available; standard 1-year treatment (Q2W or Q4W)

safety_profile.rationale: Better safety than ipilimumab; lower rate of grade 3/4 AEs; manageable immune-related adverse events; 1-year treatment duration

biomarker_match.rationale: Effective in BRAF V600E mutant melanoma; works through immune mechanism independent of BRAF pathway; broad applicability across molecular subtypes

treatments.mechanism_of_action: PD-1 checkpoint inhibitor restoring T-cell mediated anti-tumor immunity. After complete surgical resection, adjuvant nivolumab maintains immune surveillance against residual micrometastatic melanoma cells, preventing disease recurrence and distant metastasis.
biomarker_requirements: Resected stage III or IV melanoma

treatments.name: Dabrafenib plus Trametinib (Metastatic/Unresectable Disease)

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple phase 3 trials (COMBI-d, COMBI-v); 5-year OS 34%; FDA approved first-line for BRAF V600E metastatic melanoma; well-established standard

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year OS 34%; median PFS ~12 months; rapid responses suitable for symptomatic disease; 3-year PFS 22% showing durable responses in subset

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved oral medications for BRAF V600E/K metastatic melanoma; widely available worldwide

safety_profile.rationale: Pyrexia in ~50%, fatigue, nausea; dose modifications required frequently; cardiac toxicity monitoring needed; manageable for most patients

biomarker_match.rationale: BRAF V600E mutation is the direct molecular target; confirmed in this patient; provides rapid response while immunotherapy takes effect; can be used as bridge therapy

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Dual BRAF V600E + MEK1/2 inhibition provides complete blockade of constitutively activated MAPK/ERK signaling pathway. Rapid tumor shrinkage in BRAF-mutant melanoma. Combined inhibition prevents paradoxical MAPK activation and delays acquired resistance compared to BRAF inhibitor monotherapy.

treatments.name: Encorafenib plus Binimetinib

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 COLUMBUS trial; FDA approved for BRAF V600E/K metastatic melanoma; comparable efficacy to dabrafenib+trametinib in real-world analysis; established alternative

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 14.9 months; median OS 33.6 months; similar to dabrafenib+trametinib (PFS 7.9 vs 8.0 months, OS 15.5 vs 15.4 months in real-world matched analysis)

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved oral medications for BRAF V600E/K metastatic melanoma; widely available; alternative option if dabrafenib+trametinib not tolerated

safety_profile.rationale: Potentially better tolerability than dabrafenib+trametinib; lower pyrexia rates; different toxicity profile may benefit patients intolerant of dabrafenib-associated fevers

biomarker_match.rationale: BRAF V600E mutation is the direct target; confirmed in this patient; encorafenib has longer BRAF half-life potentially improving pathway suppression

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Encorafenib is a BRAF inhibitor with a longer half-life on BRAF V600E kinase compared to dabrafenib or vemurafenib, potentially providing more sustained pathway suppression. Binimetinib is a MEK1/2 inhibitor that prevents pathway reactivation. Combined BRAF+MEK blockade provides comprehensive MAPK pathway inhibition.

treatments.name: Nivolumab plus Ipilimumab (Advanced/Metastatic Disease)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 CheckMate 067: 6.5-year median OS 72 months; landmark trial for metastatic melanoma; established standard of care; DREAMseq showed superiority over targeted therapy

survival_benefit.rationale: 6.5-year median OS 72 months vs 37 months nivolumab alone vs 20 months ipilimumab alone; 5-year OS 52%; superior to BRAF-targeted therapy in DREAMseq trial (2-year OS 72% vs 52%)

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved combination for unresectable/metastatic melanoma; widely available; different dosing schedules available (standard and flat-dose)

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3/4 AEs in 59%; hepatitis, colitis, endocrinopathies common; treatment discontinuation ~40%; requires experienced immune-oncology management; long-term immune sequelae possible

biomarker_match.rationale: DREAMseq showed nivo+ipi superior to dabrafenib+trametinib in BRAF V600E melanoma; immune mechanism independent of BRAF status; more durable responses than targeted therapy

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Dual checkpoint blockade: nivolumab blocks PD-1 and ipilimumab blocks CTLA-4, activating complementary immune pathways. Combined blockade produces synergistic anti-tumor immunity greater than either agent alone. Generates durable responses through establishment of immunological memory.

treatments.name: T-VEC (Talimogene Laherparepvec) for In-Transit Metastases

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 OPTiM trial; FDA approved 2015 for unresectable in-transit cutaneous/subcutaneous melanoma; real-world data in anti-PD-1 refractory disease; direct applicability to in-transit metastases

survival_benefit.rationale: Durable response rate 16.3% vs 2.1% GM-CSF in OPTiM; responses seen in injected and non-injected lesions (abscopal effect); real-world activity in PD-1-refractory disease

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for injectable cutaneous melanoma lesions; available at specialized melanoma centers; direct intralesional administration

safety_profile.rationale: Well-tolerated with mainly local reactions; flu-like symptoms; no systemic immune-related AEs typical of checkpoint inhibitors; suitable for elderly and comorbid patients

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets accessible in-transit metastases; induces both local tumor lysis and systemic anti-tumor immunity; applicable regardless of BRAF status; scalp location facilitates injection access

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Modified herpes simplex virus type 1 selectively replicates in tumor cells, causing oncolysis and releasing tumor antigens. Also expresses GM-CSF to recruit and activate dendritic cells, generating systemic anti-tumor immunity. Produces both local (injected lesion) and abscopal (distant lesion) responses through immune activation.
biomarker_requirements: Injectable cutaneous or subcutaneous melanoma lesions

treatments.name: Wide Local Excision with Sentinel Lymph Node Biopsy

evidence_level.rationale: Gold standard surgical approach for primary melanoma; NCCN/ESMO guidelines recommend 2cm margins for T4 tumors; well-established staging and therapeutic procedure

survival_benefit.rationale: Surgery alone insufficient for stage IIIC; adjuvant therapy essential; complete resection required before adjuvant/neoadjuvant therapy; challenging on scalp due to limited tissue

accessibility.rationale: Standard surgical procedure widely available; scalp reconstruction may require specialized plastic surgery consultation

safety_profile.rationale: Standard surgical risks; scalp wound healing generally good; may require skin grafting for large defects; cosmetic concerns manageable with scalp prosthetics

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-driven; necessary regardless of molecular profile; provides tissue for staging and molecular profiling

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Complete surgical excision of primary melanoma with adequate margins (2cm for T4 tumors) removes gross disease. Sentinel lymph node biopsy provides accurate staging. For stage IIIC with known in-transit mets, completion lymph node dissection may be considered but is increasingly replaced by adjuvant systemic therapy.

key_evidence.study_name: NCCN Guidelines for Cutaneous Melanoma Surgical Management

treatments.name: Intralesional T-VEC plus Topical Imiquimod

evidence_level.rationale: Case reports and small series demonstrating successful treatment of in-transit metastatic melanoma; innovative combination approach; limited prospective data

survival_benefit.rationale: Complete responses reported in in-transit disease; dual immunostimulatory approach; potential for durable local and systemic responses

accessibility.rationale: Both T-VEC and imiquimod available; T-VEC FDA approved, imiquimod FDA approved for other indications; combination used off-label

safety_profile.rationale: Local therapy with minimal systemic toxicity; injection site reactions and local inflammation expected; no major systemic adverse events

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets accessible in-transit metastases regardless of BRAF status; dual innate (imiquimod TLR7 agonism) and adaptive (T-VEC) immune activation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: T-VEC provides oncolytic viral-mediated tumor destruction and GM-CSF-driven immune activation. Topical imiquimod activates TLR7 on dendritic cells and macrophages, enhancing innate immunity and promoting Th1 anti-tumor responses. Combined approach provides synergistic local immune activation for in-transit melanoma metastases.

key_evidence.study_name: Combination T-VEC and Imiquimod for In-Transit Melanoma

treatments.name: Lifileucel (Amtagvi) TIL Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved February 2024 (accelerated approval) for unresectable/metastatic melanoma after anti-PD-1 and BRAF inhibitor; ORR ~30% in heavily pretreated patients; first TIL therapy approved for solid tumors

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR ~30% with ~40% of responders progression-free at 1 year; durable responses with single infusion; meaningful benefit in heavily pretreated, checkpoint-refractory patients

accessibility.rationale: Limited to specialized centers; complex manufacturing process (patient's tumor resected, TILs expanded, re-infused); requires lymphodepleting chemotherapy; limited availability and high cost

safety_profile.rationale: Requires lymphodepleting chemotherapy (cyclophosphamide/fludarabine); cytokine release syndrome; prolonged cytopenias; hospitalization required; IL-2 administration adds toxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: Applicable to BRAF V600E patients after progression on both checkpoint inhibitors and BRAF/MEK inhibitors; represents salvage option; tumor-specific T cells may overcome prior treatment resistance

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs) are harvested from the patient's resected tumor, expanded ex vivo to billions of cells, and re-infused after lymphodepleting chemotherapy. These autologous T cells recognize tumor-specific antigens and mount a targeted immune response. Single infusion can produce durable responses through establishment of anti-tumor immunological memory.
biomarker_requirements: Prior BRAF inhibitor (if BRAF V600 positive)

treatments.name: Electrochemotherapy for In-Transit Metastases

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple prospective studies; response rates up to 85% for melanoma skin metastases; ESOPE guidelines established; standard in Europe, growing in US

survival_benefit.rationale: Local control modality; high response rates (up to 85%) but limited systemic anti-tumor effect; palliative benefit for symptomatic in-transit disease

accessibility.rationale: Available at specialized centers; requires electroporation equipment; more widely available in Europe than North America

safety_profile.rationale: Well-tolerated local procedure; minimal systemic toxicity; can be performed under local anesthesia; suitable for repeated treatments

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-driven; applicable to any injectable cutaneous/subcutaneous melanoma lesion; scalp accessibility may facilitate treatment

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Electroporation uses electrical pulses to transiently permeabilize tumor cell membranes, dramatically increasing intracellular uptake of chemotherapy (typically bleomycin or cisplatin) by 1000-fold. This converts normally non-permeable cytotoxic drugs into highly effective anti-tumor agents at the local level.

clinical_trials.title: NADINA: Neoadjuvant Nivolumab + Ipilimumab vs Adjuvant Nivolumab in Resectable Stage III Melanoma
clinical_trials.early_results: 12-month EFS 83.7% neoadjuvant vs 57.2% adjuvant (HR 0.32); 59% MPR rate; 2-year EFS 77.3% vs 55.7%

clinical_trials.title: SWOG S1801: Neoadjuvant/Adjuvant vs Adjuvant-Only Pembrolizumab for Resectable Stage III-IV Melanoma
Dabrafenib + Trametinib + Spartalizumab for Stage IIIB/C/D BRAF V600 Melanoma
clinical_trials.early_results: Triplet combination of BRAF/MEK inhibitor + PD-1 antibody; enrollment ongoing

clinical_trials.title: Lifileucel (TIL) + Pembrolizumab as First-Line for Advanced Melanoma
clinical_trials.early_results: First-line TIL + checkpoint inhibitor combination; enrollment initiated 2024

clinical_trials.title: T-VEC + Nivolumab for In-Transit and Stage IIIB-IV Melanoma
clinical_trials.early_results: Combination of intralesional oncolytic virus with systemic checkpoint inhibitor showing enhanced response rates

clinical_trials.title: Electrochemotherapy Combined with Checkpoint Inhibitors for In-Transit Melanoma
clinical_trials.early_results: Investigating whether local ECT can enhance systemic immunotherapy response through immunogenic cell death

combination_strategies.rationale: Neoadjuvant checkpoint blockade with intact tumor provides maximal immune priming; pathological response guides adjuvant therapy decisions; 68% reduction in recurrence/death vs adjuvant alone
Triple combination of BRAF/MEK inhibition + PD-1 blockade aims to combine rapid targeted therapy response with durable immunotherapy benefit in BRAF V600E patients

Intralesional T-VEC induces local tumor lysis and immune activation; combined with systemic checkpoint blockade to convert local to systemic anti-tumor response; addresses both in-transit and distant micrometastatic disease
Sequential approach: checkpoint inhibitors first-line, with TIL therapy as salvage for checkpoint-refractory disease; TIL provides tumor-specific T cells that may overcome prior checkpoint resistance
ECT causes immunogenic cell death releasing tumor antigens; combined with systemic checkpoint inhibitors may enhance abscopal responses and improve both local and systemic disease control

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Guidelines for Stage III Melanoma; scalp melanoma has high risk of brain metastases
supportive_care.benefit: Scalp melanoma has elevated risk of CNS metastases due to anatomic proximity and rich vascular drainage; regular brain MRI enables early detection and treatment of brain metastases with SRS or surgery

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Guidelines; AAD melanoma follow-up recommendations
supportive_care.benefit: Regular full-body skin examinations detect new primary melanomas, in-transit metastases, and satellite lesions early; scalp requires careful examination; 3-6 month intervals recommended for stage IIIC

Structured monitoring for immune-related hepatitis, colitis, endocrinopathies, and pneumonitis enables early intervention; particularly critical with nivolumab+ipilimumab combination where grade 3/4 AEs occur in ~30-59%

Stage IIIC melanoma diagnosis with in-transit metastases causes significant psychological distress; professional counseling, support groups, and mind-body practices improve coping and treatment adherence

supportive_care.evidence: AAD and NCCN melanoma prevention guidelines
supportive_care.benefit: Strict UV avoidance reduces risk of new primary melanomas; scalp protection with hats and sunscreen essential; UV exposure also associated with melanoma immunosuppression

supportive_care.evidence: ACSM Roundtable on Exercise and Cancer; multiple meta-analyses
supportive_care.benefit: Regular moderate exercise (150 min/week) improves treatment tolerance, reduces fatigue, enhances immune function, and may improve cancer outcomes; adapted programs for patients on immunotherapy
